era_context: Identical to -1630, -1631, and -1632: Late Bronze Age, 18th Dynasty Egypt, Hittite consolidation, Shang Dynasty China.

events.title: 18th Dynasty Egyptian rule
events.description: The 18th Dynasty continues its imperial administration. This year falls within the reign of Thutmose III or his successor. The Egyptian state maintains its control over the Nile Valley, Nubia, and the Levant through military and administrative means.

sources.name: Egyptian temple inscriptions and administrative records

events.title: Hittite empire consolidation
events.description: The Hittite Empire continues its consolidation and expansion. The state maintains its military and administrative structures and engages in diplomatic relations with neighboring powers.

sources.name: Hittite royal inscriptions and administrative texts

events.title: Shang Dynasty oracle bone divination
events.description: The Shang Dynasty continues its oracle bone divination practices. These bones record the concerns and activities of the Shang court.
events.certainty_note: Shang chronology remains debated; uncertainty ±50–100 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for -1633 distinct from -1630, -1631, and -1632.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1633 is essentially identical to -1630, -1631, and -1632. Individual years in this period are archaeologically and textually indistinguishable.
